metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before implementing a new composition hook in passes.py, confirm whether the existing `variant_of` + `<!--base-->` overlay system (prompts.py:337, 460–484) already covers the shared-preamble injection use case — if so, prefer extending the overlay mechanism over adding a new runtime layer.
findings.finding: The proposed 'shared-preamble composition point' in passes.py/prompt resolution rebuilds functionality already provided by the codebase's variant_of + <!--base--> overlay mechanism (prompts.py:337, 460-484), which lets a parent prompt body be spliced into child prompts at load time — without any new code layer.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive' and 'must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)'; AC line 1 — 'Shared-preamble composition point introduced'
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `plan-review-shared-preamble.md` base prompt carrying the injection directive and forward-looking rule. Add `variant_of: plan-review-shared-preamble` + `<!--base-->` to the 41 plan_review_*.md files (or the shared pass prompts). No new code layer needed; the existing overlay splice handles composition at load time via the current load_prompt path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale to the plan explaining why the Python-layer `_resolve_system` composition seam is chosen over the existing prompt variant/overlay mechanism (`<!--base-->` marker), or reconsider using the overlay mechanism to keep the change entirely in prompt files with no new Python seam.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new Python-layer composition seam inside `_resolve_system` (passes.py) to prepend the shared preamble, but the codebase already has a prompt variant/overlay mechanism (`<!--base-->` marker + `<id>.<variant>.md` or front-matter `variant_of`) that composes prompt text without touching Python. Placing the preamble in a `plan_review_finder.base.md` (or as a direct edit to `plan_review_finder.md` and other relevant pass prompts) using the existing overlay mechanism achieves the same 'write once, applies everywhere' goal with no new Python seam, better codebase alignment, and narrower blast radius. The plan does not provide a rationale for why the Python-layer approach is preferred.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive' and 'FP-3(c) forward-looking rule: hoist to the shared preamble'; `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:237-251` (`_resolve_system`); `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py:433-483` (`load_prompt` variant mechanism)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before implementing the new passes.py composition seam, evaluate whether the existing `load_prompt` variant/overlay system (`variant_of` + `<!--base-->` marker) can deliver the shared preamble to all 41 criterion prompts without new runtime infrastructure; document the comparison and justify the chosen approach.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel shared-preamble composition point in passes.py but does not consider the already-existing prompt-variant/overlay system (load_prompt with `variant_of` + `<!--base-->` marker in prompts.py) as a prior-art approach to the same goal. A shared `.variant.md` file inherited by all 41 criterion prompts via `variant_of` could place the injection directive and forward-looking rule once with no new runtime infrastructure in passes.py.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive … this story must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)'; AC1 — 'Shared-preamble composition point introduced'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to 'What' titled 'Why not the existing variant/overlay system': either show that variant_of cannot satisfy the requirement (e.g. because criterion prompts have no shared base registered in the catalog, or because the variant mechanism's merge semantics conflict with required-variable enforcement) and then proceed with the passes.py seam, or adopt the variant approach and drop the passes.py infrastructure work from AC1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the back-out section to explicitly name BOTH artifacts to revert (passes.py composition-layer change AND the 41 prompt-file edits), and verify the AC's 'revert' clause covers both.
findings.finding: The back-out section says 'revert the prompt-edit commit' but the plan also introduces a structural code change to passes.py (shared-preamble composition point). Reverting only the prompt-edit commit does not undo the passes.py change, leaving the composition-layer behavioral change without an explicit rollback path. The AC's 'If it regresses: revert' is similarly ambiguous about scope. This is a cross-section contradiction between the back-out description and the actual scope of changes.
findings.location: What section (back-out bullet) vs AC line 3 ('If it regresses: revert') vs What section (shared-preamble composition point in passes.py)
findings.suggested_fix: Update the back-out bullet to: 'Revert both the prompt-edit commit AND the passes.py shared-preamble commit (two separate revert commits or a single branch revert); document in ticket. AC eval-regression clause updated to: If pass-rate regresses, revert both commits and record pre/post numbers.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic proving command for AC2 (e.g., a script or pytest fixture that scans the 41 files for paragraphs matching the 'bare DO-NOT-only' definition and asserts count == 0), or explicitly document why a manual checklist is the intended durable proof.
findings.finding: AC2's only proof mechanism is 'a checklist run recorded per file ... in the ticket' — a human-authored artifact rather than a deterministic, re-runnable command — so AC2 cannot be verified by CI or a reviewer running a command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 proof clause

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or author the exact 'forward-looking rule' text that the shared preamble must carry — it does not currently exist in plan_review_F1.md or any other plan_review_*.md file, so 'hoisting' it requires first defining it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'FP-3(c) forward-looking rule … lives ONLY in plan_review_F1.md today (E1 does not carry it),' implying this rule is present in F1.md. Reading plan_review_F1.md confirms it contains no 'forward-looking' rule, directive, or constraint — the rule does not exist anywhere in the 41 plan_review_*.md files. The premise that drives the 'hoist to shared preamble' work item is unverifiable.
findings.location: What section: 'FP-3(c) forward-looking rule: hoist to the shared preamble. CORRECTION: it lives ONLY in plan_review_F1.md today'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Author the FP-3(c) forward-looking rule text (draft: …); it does not yet exist in any prompt file. The preamble introduction will be its first placement, not a hoist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the actual text (or quote it from its source) of the G-12 prompt-injection directive that should appear in the shared preamble, so the AC1 deliverable is self-contained.
findings.finding: The G-12 'injection directive' is the primary content item for the shared preamble, but its value vocabulary (actual text, format, or behavioral specification) is never defined anywhere in the plan. The plan explicitly states 'G-12' is a provenance tag 'NOT a codebase symbol and cannot be grepped,' and no codebase artifact defines it. An executor cannot write the preamble without knowing what the directive says.
findings.location: What section, G-12 bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under 'What' that quotes the injection directive verbatim, or link to the exact section of `~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md` where G-12 is defined and paste the relevant text inline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Quote the FP-3(c) forward-looking rule text from plan_review_F1.md inline in the plan (or identify the exact sentence(s)) so the hoist operation and the removal from F1.md are unambiguous.
findings.finding: The FP-3(c) 'forward-looking rule' is the second content item for the shared preamble, but its actual text is never quoted or specified in the plan. The plan only states it 'lives ONLY in plan_review_F1.md today (E1 does not carry it)' — no definition of what the rule says is given. Confirmed: plan_review_F1.md exists but the plan never quotes the relevant passage.
findings.location: What section, FP-3(c) bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Read plan_review_F1.md, identify the forward-looking rule sentence(s), and include them verbatim in the plan's 'What' section under the FP-3(c) bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the AC whether `test_plan_review_evals.py` runs against a live (or sufficiently faithful local) LLM, and if it uses an offline substitute, add a rationale explaining why that substitute is faithful to production recall behavior — or add a named live/end-to-end acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: The eval regression proof ('pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py pass-rate >= baseline') does not clarify whether the eval harness runs against a live LLM or an offline/canned substitute. If the tests are purely offline (snapshot, fixture, or stub-LLM), newly-reworded prompts could pass the suite while silently regressing in real LLM recall — a proxy-validation risk for the primary behavioral change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval/sidecar regression' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'The eval harness uses [live LLM | local model X with documented correlation to production recall]. If offline: document why the substitute faithfully represents production LLM behavior for prompt-content changes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or supplement the AC3 proof command with one that actually measures recall: either name the eval CI job (e.g., `.github/workflows/eval-plan-review-container.yml`) and its pass-rate output, or specify a separate harness that runs prompts against the labeled dataset and emits a numeric score.
findings.finding: AC3 specifies 'Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both numbers).' Reading the file confirms it is a structural/offline test suite (spec parsing, dataset shape, gold-set presence) — it produces no pass-rate metric, records no baseline, and has no numeric comparison against a prior run. The named command cannot satisfy the stated proof.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: AC3 'Eval/sidecar regression: reworded prompts keep prior recall. Proof: pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline'
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proof: 'Run the plan-review eval CI job (eval-plan-review-container.yml) with `[eval]` extra; record the deterministic-scorer pass-rate before and after the sweep; require post >= pre. If live model is unavailable, the offline structural test is a floor check only and does not satisfy this AC.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC3's proof command with the eval-extras invocation that actually exercises the live recall harness (e.g. `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py --extras eval` or document the correct CI job name), or explicitly state that the offline test pins spec structure while a separate eval CI step pins recall.
findings.finding: AC3 names `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` as the proof artifact for 'pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline', but that test file is explicitly offline-only: its header states 'The live model run is gated behind the [eval] extra in the eval CI'. Running it without extras validates spec structure, not model recall. The pass-rate claim cannot be proved by the cited command.
findings.location: AC3 ('Eval/sidecar regression: reworded prompts keep prior recall. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline')
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -m eval` (or the project-specific eval-CI job) — record baseline recall %, rerun after sweep, require no regression.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define ownership of the shared preamble (who may edit it, what review is required) and document a retirement/migration path (e.g., in CONTRIBUTING.md) so the lifecycle is complete.
findings.finding: The shared preamble introduced in passes.py has no defined ownership or update governance: the plan does not specify who is authorized to modify the shared preamble, how changes are reviewed, or what retirement/deprecation path exists if the preamble needs to be split or removed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC line 1; What section — G-12 and FP-3(c) discussion
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section in CONTRIBUTING.md (already referenced for the checklist location) specifying that changes to the shared preamble require a full eval run and explicit sign-off, and describe how to retire the preamble if the composition pattern changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the composition hook location (function name, file, call-site in passes.py or prompts.py), its interaction with the existing variant_of/<!--base--> overlay mechanism, and name the test file that will verify AC1.
findings.finding: The shared-preamble composition point is described as being introduced 'in passes.py prompt resolution,' but the plan never specifies the mechanism: no function name, no call-site, no interaction protocol with the existing variant_of / <!--base--> overlay system (confirmed present in prompts.py:443-483). The existing system already provides a prompt-composition mechanism that the plan ignores.
findings.location: What section, G-12 bullet ('resolve_prompt loads each prompt whole... must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point'); Acceptance Criteria AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the affirmative-framing AC to explicitly include the shared pass prompts (e.g. 'count = 41 plan_review_*.md + N shared pass prompts, all verified') so no described work is left without a covering criterion.
findings.finding: The plan body scopes the affirmative-framing sweep to '41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files (+ the shared pass prompts)' but the covering AC only verifies 'count = 41, zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks' — the shared pass prompts are described work with no verification step in any AC.
findings.location: What section (Scope bullet) vs AC line 2 (affirmative-framing sweep criterion)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC line 2 to: 'Proof: a checklist run recorded per file (count = 41 plan_review_*.md files + all shared pass prompts, zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks in either set) in the ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or a named section in docs/plan-review-gate.md (or CLAUDE.md) documenting the shared-preamble composition contract in passes.py — what it prepends, why, and how future prompts should rely on it — before or alongside the implementation PR.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern — a shared-preamble composition point in passes.py that prepends a preamble to all 41 reviewer prompts — but names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to capture this decision. The AC documents only the checklist habit location (CONTRIBUTING.md / docs/plan-review-gate.md), not the prompt-resolution architecture itself.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive. CORRECTION: … must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)' + AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/0XXX-shared-reviewer-preamble.md (or append a 'Prompt Resolution Architecture' section to docs/plan-review-gate.md) covering: (1) the decision to compose preamble at resolve_prompt time rather than per-file, (2) the contents of the shared preamble (injection directive + forward-looking rule), (3) how to extend it. Reference this doc in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the shared-preamble composition point is necessary for the current 2-directive scope (not just future-proofing): if the back-out and recall-regression ACs can be satisfied by direct per-file edits + a grep-based CI check, prefer that over a new runtime composition layer.
findings.finding: The composition-point abstraction is YAGNI: its stated justification ('otherwise it degrades to per-prompt duplication') serves a hypothetical future-directive accumulation scenario, not the current done-definition, which requires placing only 2 terse directives.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive. CORRECTION: … must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point … otherwise it degrades to per-prompt duplication'; 'FP-3(c) forward-looking rule: hoist to the shared preamble'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the AC proof specification to name the correct collection (e.g. 'every packaged prompt whose id starts with `plan-review-`' or 'every file in `reviewers/plan_review_*.md`') rather than 'every plan_review reviewer', which the code would interpret as zero files via `load_catalog()`.
findings.finding: The AC proof for the shared-preamble composition point says 'unit test asserts every plan_review reviewer resolves with the shared preamble prepended', but `load_catalog()` — the natural collection to iterate — filters on `category: review`. Every plan_review criterion file (plan_review_F1.md, plan_review_E1.md, etc.) declares `category: plan-review-criterion` and every pass file declares `category: plan-review-pass`, so `load_catalog()` returns zero of the 41 target files. A test written against `load_catalog()` or the `is_reviewer` predicate would trivially pass while testing the wrong (empty) set.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Shared-preamble composition point introduced' bullet; `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py:139-143` (`is_reviewer` property); representative criterion files `plan_review_F1.md` (line 8: `category: plan-review-criterion`)
findings.suggested_fix: Reference `_packaged_prompt_files()` (filtered to `plan-review-` prefix) or explicitly iterate the 41 known prompt ids — not `load_catalog()` / `is_reviewer`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'bare DO-NOT-only block' operationally in the plan (e.g., 'any paragraph whose only instruction is a negation with no affirmative alternative stated in the same block') so the 41-file sweep can be executed and verified without executor judgment.
findings.finding: The term 'bare DO-NOT-only block' used in AC2 is not operationally defined anywhere in the plan — no threshold, no example, no rule distinguishing a 'bare' prohibition from an acceptable terse one — making the 41-file sweep non-executable without an executor judgment call.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'A block is bare-DO-NOT-only when it contains ≥1 DO-NOT / never / avoid sentence and zero affirmative sentences stating what to do instead. Such blocks must be rewritten to lead with the affirmative rule; if a prohibition must be retained, it must be followed immediately by the affirmative equivalent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'bare DO-NOT-only block' operationally (e.g., 'a paragraph or sentence that contains a DO-NOT/NEVER/do not instruction with no adjacent affirmative alternative in the same sentence or immediately following sentence') so the AC2 count is reproducible.
findings.finding: The 'bare DO-NOT-only block' — the unit of measurement for the affirmative-framing sweep (AC2) — is never operationally defined. The plan says 'zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks' is the proof condition, but no rule distinguishes a 'bare' prohibition (failing) from an acceptable terse prohibition with an adjacent 'do this instead' (passing under R-6).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add one passing example ('DO NOT flag X; instead, flag only Y') and one failing example ('DO NOT flag X.') to the R-6 bullet in the What section, and reference this definition in AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a behavioral proof for the affirmative-framing sweep: e.g., sample spot-check runs (or a diff review gate) verifying that at least a representative subset of reworded prompts produce equivalent or better recall in a live/faithful eval before the sweep is closed.
findings.finding: The AC proof for the affirmative-framing sweep ('a checklist run recorded per file, count = 41, zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks') is a structural text-pattern check, not a behavioral test. It verifies absence of a syntactic pattern but cannot confirm the reworded prompts are semantically equivalent or actually affirmative in effect — the behavioral safety net relies entirely on the eval regression whose faithfulness is already in question (see E5 proxy-validation finding).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Affirmative-framing sweep complete' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/fidelity_spot_eval.py` (which calls `resolve_prompt` directly, bypassing `_resolve_system`) to the plan's enumerated consumers requiring update when the shared-preamble composition point is introduced.
findings.finding: The plan names `passes.py` as the location for the new preamble composition point but does not enumerate consumers of `_resolve_system` outside `passes.py` that may need updating. `fidelity_spot_eval.py` also calls `prompts.resolve_prompt` directly (lines 99, 109) and is a named consumer of the same prompt machinery — if the preamble is injected in `_resolve_system` only, `fidelity_spot_eval.py` will resolve prompts WITHOUT the preamble, violating AC1's 'every plan_review reviewer resolves with the shared preamble prepended' assertion.
findings.location: What section ('introduce a shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)'); AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the location choice (commit to CONTRIBUTING.md OR docs/plan-review-gate.md, not 'or'), state the observable outcome (e.g., 'a named section heading exists in <file>'), and add a proving command (e.g., `grep -n 'Plan-Review Checklist' CONTRIBUTING.md`).
findings.finding: AC4 ('Review-time checklist location documented') is effort-framed and states no observable outcome or proving command — it is unclear what artifact would exist, where to find it, or what command verifies it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC4 with: 'A "Plan-Review Checklist" section exists in CONTRIBUTING.md. Proof: `grep -c "Plan-Review Checklist" CONTRIBUTING.md` returns ≥ 1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the 'shared pass prompts' files by name or glob pattern in the plan's scope definition so the executor can act without investigation.
findings.finding: The scope phrase 'shared pass prompts' is never resolved to concrete file paths, leaving the executor to determine which files beyond the 41 plan_review_*.md files are in scope for the preamble insertion and affirmative-framing sweep.
findings.location: What section — 'Scope = the 41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files (+ the shared pass prompts)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for the shared-preamble composition path in passes.py: define whether a missing/malformed preamble raises loudly, logs a warning, or falls back — and add at least one log/assertion so the failure is observable.
findings.finding: The shared-preamble composition point in passes.py has no described error handling: if preamble loading/composition fails, the plan does not specify whether the error is surfaced, logged, or causes a graceful degradation — prompts could silently resolve without the injection directive or forward-looking rule, defeating the security and correctness goals of the story with no observable signal.
findings.location: What section — 'G-12 prompt-injection directive' and 'shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)'
findings.suggested_fix: In passes.py preamble resolution: raise a clear exception (or log ERROR + re-raise) if the preamble file cannot be read, so the failure is never swallowed. The existing unit test (AC line 1) then also serves as a load-time guard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the proposed composition point will be exercised by >=3 distinct call-sites (including the workflow RunnerAgentStep path), or document why a single-call-site indirection is justified over direct per-file edits.
findings.finding: The new shared-preamble composition point in passes.py has only one identifiable call-site (_resolve_system, passes.py:237-251), falling short of the Rule-of-Three threshold for a justified abstraction.
findings.location: What section — 'shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution)'; AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all callers of `_resolve_system` (and any other `resolve_prompt` consumers in `passes.py`) that will be affected when the shared-preamble composition point is introduced, and explicitly classify the composition-point introduction as a new behavioral hunk in the plan's scope section.
findings.finding: The shared-preamble composition point is pure new creation: no such layer exists anywhere in the codebase (neither in passes.py nor prompts.py), yet AC1 treats it as verifiable via a unit test. The plan correctly identifies this as net-new work in the 'What' section, but the behavioral hunk (new prepend logic in passes.py/_resolve_system or prompts.py/resolve_prompt) is an out-of-scope CREATION that is unacknowledged as such in the AC.
findings.location: What section (G-12 bullet); AC1 ('Shared-preamble composition point introduced')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either confirm `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` already exists (point to it) or add its creation as an explicit deliverable of this story with its own AC entry covering the baseline measurement step.
findings.finding: The regression proof AC (`pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py`) references a test file that does not exist in the codebase. The file is absent from `tests/unit/` and no search finds it. An implementer following the plan has no runnable baseline to record before the sweep, and the AC's 'record both numbers' instruction is unexecutable until the file is created — but the plan never calls this out as a deliverable of the story.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval/sidecar regression' bullet; `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
